Rhodiola rosea, also known as Rhodax cinereus (though that's likely a misspelling), is an adaptogenic plant that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of Rhodiola rosea include reducing stress and anxiety, improving mood and cognitive function, and enhancing physical performance. The bioactive constituents of Rhodiola rosea include rosavin, rosin, and salidroside, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rhodiola rosea include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to promote relaxation and improve mental performance.
